channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kwanzahub: Supporting Tanzanian Youth to Innovate and Earn While Waiting for Jobs
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, a new innovation platform called Kwanzahub is bringing hope for young innovators and recent graduates who are struggling to find formal jobs. Founded by Mihayo Wilmore, a c...

item.title: Centre cites health risks for restricting blood donation among high-risk groups
item.description: The Union Government has explained its decision to exclude transgender individuals, men who have sex with men (MSM), and female sex workers from the list of eligible blood donors. Additional Solicitor...

item.title: ANC explores potential collaboration with DA in Gauteng to stabilise municipalities
item.description: The ANC in Gauteng is considering collaborating with the Democratic Alliance (DA) in the provincial legislature as a way to ensure stability in municipalities such as Johannesburg and Ekurhuleni. This...

item.title: Nyoro Questions NTSA Sh42 Billion Automated Traffic Camera System, Raises Concerns on Motives
item.description: Kiharu MP Ndindi Nyoro has raised serious concerns about the newly launched NTSA automated traffic enforcement system, a project worth Sh42 billion. According to Nyoro, the system, which uses about 1,...

item.title: CS Mutahi Kagwe Attends Son's Beach Wedding in Watamu Alongside Former President Uhuru Kenyatta
item.description: Cabinet Secretary for Agriculture and Livestock Development, Mutahi Kagwe, together with his wife Ann Wanjiku, proudly walked their son Njoroge Mutahi down the aisle during a beautiful beach wedding h...

item.title: Philippine government agencies shift to four-day workweek to reduce costs
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — In line with President Marcos' directive to reduce operational costs amid the Middle East conflict, several key Philippine government agencies are adopting a shorter, four-day wo...

item.title: Paramedic intervenes to stop intoxicated driver on tram tracks in Nottingham
item.description: Levi Gomez, a 27-year-old from St Ann's, Nottingham, was recorded driving along the tram tracks at University Boulevard near the Queen's Medical Centre in the early hours of 6 April last year. CCTV fo...

item.title: Claudia Winkleman prepares to launch her new BBC chat show amid career high
item.description: Claudia Winkleman, the popular host of The Traitors and former co-host of Strictly Come Dancing, is set to debut her own eponymous chat show on BBC One on Friday 13 March 2026. The programme, produced...

item.title: Kilimanjaro region kicks off first volleyball league with 19 teams
item.description: Kilimanjaro region is setting a big milestone in sports with the launch of its very first volleyball league, the Kilimanjaro Volleyball League 2026 (KVL26). A total of 19 teams will participate, with ...

item.title: Trial Continues for the Men Accused of Killing Soccer Star Senzo Meyiwa
item.description: The trial of the five men accused of murdering renowned South African footballer Senzo Meyiwa has resumed at the Pretoria High Court. Meyiwa, a celebrated goalkeeper and captain of Bafana Bafana, was ...

item.title: SERAP Files Case Against FG Over Mass Phone Surveillance Rules
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don carry matter go ECOWAS Community Court of Justice against the Nigerian government under President Bola Tinubu because dem never withdra...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Deaths of Two Individuals Near Riverside Courthouse
item.description: Early Thursday morning, two individuals were found dead outside the Riverside County Courthouse in downtown Riverside, California. Riverside Police responded to reports of a possible stabbing at appro...

item.title: NMG's Enduring Legacy in Kenyan Journalism
item.description: The Nation Media Group (NMG) has been a pillar in Kenyan media for decades, standing tall through thick and thin. This opinion piece highlights how NMG has maintained its position as a trusted voice d...

item.title: Australian Government Orders Non-Essential Officials to Leave Israel and UAE Due to Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Australian government has instructed all non-essential officials to leave Israel and the United Arab Emirates, citing the worsening security situation in the Middle East following the US and Israe...

item.title: Empowering women entrepreneurs in Kenya's green economy
item.description: The shift towards a green economy is picking up speed globally, with sectors like renewable energy, climate-smart agriculture, and circular production gaining traction. However, women entrepreneurs in...

item.title: Odisha village gripped by anxiety as Iran-US-Israel war affects Gulf migrant workers
item.description: In Manikapur village of Ganjam district, Odisha, nearly every household has a family member working in Gulf countries. The ongoing war in West Asia between Iran and the US-Israel alliance, starting la...

item.title: Celebrating Eid Amid Global Conflict and Resilience
item.description: As the crescent moon signals the arrival of Eid-ul-Fitr, Muslims worldwide, including in South Africa, celebrate this important festival in the shadow of war and conflict. From Gaza to Tehran, million...

item.title: Tanzania to gain from Lifezone’s Burundi nickel agreement and SGR expansion
item.description: On 10th March 2026, Lifezone Metals, which owns Tembo Nickel, signed a 14-month exclusive deal with the Government of Burundi for the Musongati nickel deposit. The deposit, sitting about 200 km south-...

item.title: B.C. First Nations chiefs respond to MP Aaron Gunn's criticism of land acknowledgments
item.description: The chiefs of four First Nations in British Columbia — the Tla'amin, Homalco, K'omoks, and Klahoose — have responded to Conservative MP Aaron Gunn after he criticized land acknowledgments at public ev...

item.title: Prince and Princess of Wales Participate in Coffee and Cheese Activities at London Borough Market
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales made a lively appearance at London Borough Market, engaging directly with vendors and visitors. Princess Kate demonstrated her barista skills at the Change Please coff...
